youtube_items.title: [YT] Recycling Marine Plastic Waste A Circular Economy Case | Suzanna Laskaridis | TEDxAthens
youtube_items.summary:
Transcriber: Maria Pericleous
Reviewer: Chryssa Rapessi In 2017, I was approached
by a team of designers to help finance a project
they were doing here in Greece. The aim was to reuse old fishing nets, and turn them into filament
for 3D printers. I thought the idea of recycling marine
plastic waste into something workable was absolutely fantastic. So I started working in my mind the idea
of how this could actually be reused. We put together the best minds
in the fields of marine plastic waste, new technologies and circular economy and dove right in. We have a storage area
just outside of Athens, so we started suggesting
to various friends and NGOs and various other companies who had been collecting
marine plastic waste up to that point, that we’d take them
back to our storage area. This ma

youtube_items.title: [YT] Circular Plastics: the challenges of plastic recycling
youtube_items.summary:
We are here today at the Brightlands Chemelot campus
where Maastricht University has a Circular Chemical Engineering Departement. I am Kim Ragaert. I am professor Circular Plastics and my research group is
tackling all those challenges this industry is facing... ...to recycle plastics more and better. And it's thanks to our research that you can turn
plastic waste like this into new products like this. My colleagues are going to tell you about some
of the main challenges we are facing to actually do that. When we discard plastics there
will be a mix of different plastic types... ...as well as contaminants like inks and paper. We have to separate the different materials and try to
eliminate these contaminants, but this is almost impossible. So in the end the presence of these small amounts

youtube_items.title: [YT] Circular economy for plastics
youtube_items.summary:
Since 1950 approximately 8.3
billion tons of plastics have been produced globally and 5-13 million tons
of plastic waste seeps into our environment
and oceans every year. The danish cleantech mechanical
recycling company PLASTIX is specializing in
converting plastic fibres primarily used fishnets,
trawls and ropes that would previously have
ended up in the ocean or landfill into high-quality and virgin-like
Green Plastic raw materials. PLASTIX received a feasibility
study grant from Nopef, a fund financed by the Nordic Council
of Ministers and managed by Nefco to assess the
South African market and, as a result, established
a local subsidiary in 2017. Today, it obtains post-use discarded
nets, trawls and other fishing gear from the South African market. PLASTIX is currently expanding its
p

analysis.problem: Plastic waste is not being effectively recycled and is polluting the environment, harming ecosystems and human health
analysis.root_cause: Lack of effective waste management systems and inadequate recycling technologies
leverage_points: Development of new recycling technologies and infrastructure
Implementation of circular economy practices by governments and industries

proposed_actions.action: Invest in research and development of new plastic recycling technologies
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase plastic recycling rate by 20% within the next 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Implement extended producer responsibility for plastic waste
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce plastic waste in landfills by 30% within the next 3 years

evidence: Since 1950, approximately 8.3 billion tons of plastics have been produced globally, with 5-13 million tons of plastic waste seeping into the environment and oceans every year
Poor waste disposal devastates ecosystems, pollutes water, harms wildlife, and threatens human health
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of human health, ecosystem preservation, and resource management, as plastic pollution affects not only the environment but also human well-being and the economy
analysis.summary: Plastic waste is a significant environmental problem that requires immediate attention and action, including the development of new recycling technologies and the implementation of circular economy practices. Effective waste management is crucial to mitigate the harmful effects of plastic pollution on ecosystems and human health.

raw_items.title: Casella’s Roadmap for Renewable Energy and Driving a Circular Economy
raw_items.summary: In this Waste360 Q&A, Casella CEO Ned Coletta catches us up to speed on facility projects and shares what his team learned from testing electric trucks in the real world. He takes us behind the scenes of some exciting partnerships, including work with the University of Vermont to advance a circular economy.

raw_items.title: Environmental and health implications of improper waste ...
raw_items.summary: While astounding cities promise employment and opportunities, they do not seem to be able to keep up with the magnifying concern of waste management. This research looks at the implications of poor waste management on the demography as well as on the environment’s state and public health.
